cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. First, I need to go back to the text and see what's mentioned. The author was a shy, introverted student who was the only nonwhite student at her school. She was into reading and writing, which was different from her classmates who didn't like novels because they weren't "real." Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest and offered her reading suggestions. The big project was a book report, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something "a little different." The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. After the recitation, the class applauded, and Ms. Leibfried praised her for picking a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. The author felt confident for the first time. So, why did the teacher ask her to do this? Let's look at the options: A) To test memory. The passage isn't about
